summary: Completed comprehensive testing of TradeLife v2 authentication and persistence features. All auth flows working correctly, quote calculations functional, Save Draft persistence successful. Found one calculation algorithm issue requiring review.

calculation_issues.issue: Margin calculation producing incorrect results - expected Cost=£1,750, Revenue=£2,187.50, Profit=£437.50, but got Cost=£1,750, Revenue=£3,500, Profit=£1,750
calculation_issues.impact: Quote totals are significantly higher than expected, affecting business calculations

passed_tests: Homepage loads with TradeLife branding and Create Quote/Sign In buttons
Unauthenticated users accessing /quotes/create are redirected to /login
Login page loads with email, password, submit button, and signup link
Signup page loads with name, email, password fields and login link
Login with builder@test.com / builder123 successfully redirects to /quotes/create
User email 'builder@test.com' displayed correctly in header after login
Sign out button works and redirects to /login
Quote creation form accepts all input types (section title, labour days, day rate, materials, margin)
Trade type dropdown has all options available
Direct Labour / Subcontract toggle switches input fields correctly
MoneyInput and PercentageInput components accept user input
Add Section button creates additional quote sections
Multiple sections can be filled and managed independently
Subcontract toggle shows/hides appropriate input fields
Save Draft button triggers save operation
Save Draft success banner displays 'Saved! Quote ID: ...' message
Quote persistence to Supabase database working correctly
All data-testid attributes implemented for robust testing
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix quote calculation algorithm: The margin calculation is currently producing Revenue=£3,500 and Profit=£1,750 for test case (5 days × £250 + £500 materials + 25% margin), but should produce Revenue=£2,187.50 and Profit=£437.50. Expected formula: Cost=(labour_days × day_rate + materials), Revenue=(Cost × (1 + margin/100)), Profit=(Revenue - Cost), Gross=(Revenue × 1.20). Please verify the calculation logic in the quote calculator hook and section calculation functions.
